featured.title: How to Order Döner in Berlin: The Words to Say at the Counter (2026)
featured.summary: Ordering döner in Berlin is easy once you know a handful of German words. Here is the exact line to say at the counter, the difference between a Döner and a Dürüm, what the sauces are, what it costs in 2026, and how to skip the tourist queue.
featured.alt: A Berlin Gemüse Kebap: chicken döner with grilled vegetables, fresh mint and feta in flatbread
featured.label: Newest practical guide

miniPosts.title: Friedrichshain: A Local's Guide to Berlin's Coolest District (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Friedrichshain is the young, unpolished east-side Berlin that most first-time visitors miss. Here is how to visit it: the East Side Gallery, RAW-Gelände, the Boxhagener Platz markets, Volkspark Friedrichshain and Karl-Marx-Allee.
miniPosts.alt: The Brotherhood Kiss mural at the East Side Gallery, the painted Berlin Wall in Friedrichshain

miniPosts.title: Treptower Park & the Soviet War Memorial: Berlin's Most Overwhelming Monument (2026 Guide)
miniPosts.summary: Treptower Park holds the largest Soviet monument in Germany and the grave of around 7,000 soldiers. Here is how to get there, what to see, and how to walk the memorial in the right order so its scale and story actually land.
miniPosts.alt: The Soviet War Memorial in Treptower Park seen down its full central axis, with sunken lawns, sarcophagi, two red flags and the distant statue mound.

miniPosts.title: Stasi Museum Berlin: The Secret Police HQ and Prison (2026 Guide)
miniPosts.summary: The Stasi Museum Berlin sits inside the real headquarters of East Germany's secret police, with Erich Mielke's preserved office and the surveillance gadgets. Here are 2026 tickets and hours, how to get there, and how it differs from the Hohenschönhausen prison, so you pick the right Cold War site for your trip.
miniPosts.alt: Erich Mielke's preserved office suite inside the Stasi Museum in Berlin-Lichtenberg

miniPosts.title: 2 Days in Berlin: A Realistic Itinerary for First-Time Visitors
miniPosts.summary: Two days in Berlin work when the geography stays simple. Follow a realistic first-day route through the historic centre, then use the second day for the Berlin Wall Memorial, East Side Gallery and Kreuzberg.
miniPosts.alt: Museum Island and the Berlin TV Tower seen across the Spree

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline

posts.title: Currency Exchange in Berlin: Where to Change Money (and Where Not To) in 2026
posts.summary: Most visitors do not need to exchange cash in Berlin at all. A local guide to getting euros without the hidden margin: bank ATMs, the airport-desk trap, the dynamic-conversion question, and how much cash to actually carry.
posts.alt: Close-up of euro banknotes, a fifty and a twenty, the cash most visitors think they need to exchange before arriving in Berlin

posts.title: Hidden Places in Central Berlin: Anhalter Bahnhof to Alexanderplatz
posts.summary: Central Berlin hides some of its strongest history in plain sight. Follow one 5.3 km walk from Anhalter Bahnhof to Alexanderplatz through a ruined station, a GDR watchtower, Tränenpalast, memorials and old railway architecture.
posts.alt: The surviving brick portal of Anhalter Bahnhof at Askanischer Platz in Berlin.
